PREF-01.Screen Text (Figma): Circle icon (large, centered) Left half: AI coach (face or bubble approach) Right half: Mic  "Can I talk?" "I have something to share with you."  "Do you prefer that I talk, or write on the screen to you? If you'd like me to talk, just say yes. If not, say screen."  Button: "Talk to me" (primary) Button: "Screen is fine" (secondary)
PREF-01.AI Context: SCREEN: Voice/Screen Preference ("Can I talk?") STATE: User just completed auth. First interaction with coach presence. No mic access yet.  BEHAVIOR: TTS plays one-way (broadcast, like splash). User responds by TAP only - mic hasn't been granted yet. Display two buttons: 'Talk to me' and 'Screen is fine.'  TWO INDEPENDENT SETTINGS: 1\. ai\_output\_mode (set here): Does the AI speak or write? 'voice' = TTS, 'screen' = text. 2\. mic\_permission (set at MIC-01): Can the user speak? On or off. Defaults to on if granted. These are independent. A user can have AI in screen mode but still use their mic to talk.  GOAL: Let user choose comfort level. Voice is preferred but screen is fully supported.  DO NOT: Imply voice response is available. Make screen mode feel lesser.  NEXT: Both paths -\> MIC-01.
PREF-01.AI Voice (TTS copy): "Can I talk? I have something to share with you. Do you prefer that I talk, or write on the screen? Tap 'Talk to me' or 'Screen is fine.'"
PREF-01.Voice Instructions: \[TTS plays one-way - like splash hook. Mic is NOT active yet.\] \[User responds by TAP only. Voice input not available on this screen.\] \[Pre-cached audio, \~6 sec\]
PREF-01.Expected User Response: Tap: "Talk to me" (primary button) Tap: "Screen is fine" (secondary button)  Note: Mic is NOT active on this screen. User responds by tap only. If no response after 10 seconds: default to 'voice', auto-navigate.
PREF-01.AI Response: TALK: \[Transition to MIC-01 for mic permission\]  SCREEN: "No problem. I'll write everything on screen. You can always switch to voice later in Settings."
PREF-01.System Action: 1\. Save user\_profile.ai\_output\_mode:    - 'Talk to me' -\> 'voice'    - 'Screen is fine' -\> 'screen'    - Default (no response/timeout) -\> 'voice' 2\. Navigate to MIC-01 (all paths) 3\. Log PostHog: voice\_preference {choice, input\_method}
PREF-01.Edge Cases: User doesn't respond (silence): After 10 seconds, default to 'voice' and navigate to MIC-01.  User says something unclear: Show the two buttons again.  User says "What's the difference?": "If I talk, you'll hear my voice. If screen, I'll write everything as text. Either way, you can always talk to me using the mic button."
PREF-01.Notes: NEW screen. Appears after auth, before welcome. Default = 'voice' if user doesn't choose. Two independent settings: ai\_output\_mode (voice/screen) and mic\_permission (on/off).
PREF-01.How Audio Works: Pre-recorded (~6 sec). Plays after auth. Tap response only. File: pref_can_i_talk.mp3

MIC-01.Screen Text (Figma): "I always want to give you the option to talk to me. To do that, I need access to your microphone."  Button: "Allow microphone"  \[If granted:\] "Got it. You can talk to me anytime now." \[If denied:\] "No problem. You can always type instead. If you change your mind, you can enable it in your browser settings."
MIC-01.AI Context: SCREEN: Mic Permission STATE: User chose voice preference (or default). Now requesting mic access.  BEHAVIOR: Pre-permission explanation first, then button triggers the browser prompt. This is the single biggest friction point. The explanation must earn the permission: 'I always want to give you the option to talk to me.'  IF GRANTED: Brief confirmation. Animate the mic icon. Move to welcome. IF DENIED: Graceful fallback. 'No problem. You can always type.' No dead end.  GOAL: Get mic access without pressure. Make denied feel OK.  DO NOT: Fire the browser prompt without explanation. Block the flow on denial. Guilt.  NEXT: Regardless of result -\> POST-AUTH-01 (welcome).
MIC-01.AI Voice (TTS copy): "I always want to give you the option to talk to me. To do that, I need access to your microphone."
MIC-01.Voice Instructions: \[Pre-permission text plays as TTS or displays as text (based on ai\_output\_mode)\] \[Button tap triggers browser permission prompt\] \[After granted: play 'Got it. You can talk to me anytime now.'\] \[After denied: play 'No problem. You can always type instead. Enable mic in browser settings.'\] \[If mic already granted: skip this screen entirely\]
MIC-01.Expected User Response: ALLOW: Tap "Allow microphone" -\> browser permission prompt -\> Grant  DENY: Browser prompt -\> Deny / Dismiss  ALREADY GRANTED: Skip this screen entirely
MIC-01.AI Response: GRANTED: "Got it. You can talk to me anytime now."  DENIED: "No problem. You can always type instead. If you change your mind, enable it in your browser settings."  DISMISSED: Same as denied.
MIC-01.System Action: 1\. Button triggers navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({audio: true}) 2\. On grant: set mic\_permission = true 3\. On deny: set mic\_permission = false 4\. Navigate to POST-AUTH-01 5\. Log PostHog: mic\_permission {result: granted\

POST-AUTH-01.Screen Text (Figma): Button: "I'm ready - let's go" Button: "I'll come back later"
POST-AUTH-01.AI Context: SCREEN: Welcome (coach introduction) STATE: User just completed auth. First time meeting the coach. Mic may or may not be granted (depends on PREF-01 and MIC-01 which come before this in the new flow).  BEHAVIOR: Play the welcome audio automatically (\~60 sec). This is the trust-building moment. Cover: what the app is, how check-ins work (30 sec to a couple of minutes), time framing (2-5 min setup), privacy (anonymized from day one, used for insights), and the invitation to begin. After user taps 'I'm ready', play the presence ask (\~25 sec): focus, paradox (app asking you to be present), 'talking to your phone is better than being on your phone.'  GOAL: Build trust. Set expectations. Earn the right to their time.  DO NOT: Rush. Skip privacy. Make it feel like a terms-and-conditions moment. If user taps 'I'm ready' before welcome finishes, stop welcome and play presence ask.  NEXT: After presence ask -\> ONBOARD-01 (profile setup). If 'Come back later' -\> save state, resume here on return.
POST-AUTH-01.AI Voice (TTS copy): "Welcome. I'm your AI coach inside Guided Growth, and I'm going to be with you from here on.  This isn't an app that gives you a list and hopes for the best. You'll talk to me in the morning. You'll talk to me at night. And between those two moments, the habits you want will start happening - not because you're forcing them, but because we're removing what's in the way.  This setup takes about three to five minutes. This is probably the longest we'll ever talk. After this, your check-ins are between 30 seconds and a couple of minutes, depending on how much you want to share. And those few minutes a day? They're designed to save you time - not add to your schedule. When the right habits click into place, you stop wasting energy on the things that weren't working. That's time back. Real time.  Everything you share with me - your mood, your habits, your reflections - we use all of it to give you better insights and to keep improving this experience. But here's the thing: your data is anonymized from the first second. We never connect it to you personally. So you get deep, real reflections about your life - without anyone knowing it's you. That's the best of both worlds.  When you're ready, let's begin."
POST-AUTH-01.Voice Instructions: \[Plays automatically after PREF-01 + MIC-01\] \[Name variant: If user\_profile.name exists, prefix with 'Welcome, \[Name\].' If no name, use 'Welcome.' Real-time TTS prefix, not pre-cached.\] \[\~60 sec without presence ask. Presence ask plays separately after 'I'm ready' tap (\~25 sec).\]
POST-AUTH-01.Expected User Response: Tap: "I'm ready - let's go" Tap: "I'll come back later"  No voice input on this screen.
POST-AUTH-01.AI Response: \[If 'I'm ready':\] \[Plays after user taps 'I'm ready'\]  "One thing before we start. I need you here right now. Present. Not multitasking. Not half-listening. What we're about to do is focus on you - and that deserves your full attention.  I know it sounds like a paradox - an app asking you to be present. But that's actually the goal: to help you spend less time on your phone, not more. That's why you talk to me instead of scrolling. Talking to your phone is better than being on your phone.  Let's go."  \[If 'Come back later':\] "No rush. I'll be here when you're ready."
POST-AUTH-01.System Action: 0\. User arrives here after PREF-01 + MIC-01 (voice pref + mic permission) 1\. Check user\_profile.name: if exists use it, if not use generic 1b. Check ai\_output\_mode: if 'voice', play TTS. If 'screen', display text. 2\. Play welcome audio (TTS, male) 3\. Wait for button tap 4\. 'I'm ready': play presence ask, then onboarding\_state = 'profile\_setup', navigate to ONBOARD-01 5\. 'Come back later': onboarding\_state = 'paused\_at\_welcome' 6\. Log PostHog: post\_auth\_welcome {has\_name, action, listen\_duration}
POST-AUTH-01.Edge Cases: User might tap 'I'm ready' before welcome finishes - stop welcome audio, play presence ask.  MVP: Single cloned voice. Voice/style selection post-MVP.  Welcome is \~60 sec. Presence ask is \~25 sec after tap. Total \~85 sec split across two moments.
POST-AUTH-01.Notes: THE moment. Split into two parts: welcome (trust + info) then presence ask (focus + paradox). Neither feels like a monologue.
POST-AUTH-01.How Audio Works: welcome_intro.mp3 (~60s) + welcome_presence.mp3 (~25s). Auto-navigates to ONBOARD-01 after presence ask.

ONBOARD-01.Screen Text (Figma): "Let's get to know you." "Tell us a bit about yourself, to personalize your journey."  What should I call you? - field How old are you? - slider How do you identify? - Male/Female/Other How did you hear about us? - Founder Invite / Webinar / Friend / Other  Mic button Button: "Let's Begin"
ONBOARD-01.AI Context:
SCREEN: Profile Setup (Step 1) STATE: User just heard the welcome + presence ask. They're engaged and present.  \*\*\* THIS IS THE FIRST REAL-TIME AGENT SCREEN \*\*\* Unlike screens before this (which play pre-recorded MP3s), this screen activates the Cartesia Line agent. The AI speaks LIVE through real-time TTS, not from an MP3 file. Everything the AI says on this screen is generated by GPT and spoken by Cartesia in the cloned voice.  FLOW: 1\. Screen loads. Real-time agent connection opens automatically. 2\. Agent speaks first (introduction): asks for name, age, gender, referral. 3\. User responds by voice (or taps form fields). 4\. Agent responds with the user's NAME via live TTS: 'Great to meet you, \[Name\].'    This is the key test - the AI says the user's actual name back to them in the cloned voice. 5\. If user gave partial info, agent asks for remaining fields only. 6\. Form auto-fills in real-time as user speaks.  BEHAVIOR: Accept voice or taps. Auto-fill form fields as voice is parsed. If user gives partial info (just name), ask for remaining fields only - never re-ask what you already got. Confirm with a warm greeting using their name.  PARSING: 'Call me Sam' = name Sam. 'Twenty-five' = age 25. 'Guy/man/boy' = Male. 'Girl/woman/lady' = Female. 'Non-binary' = Other. 'TikTok/IG' = Social media. 'A friend' = Friend. 'Google' = Website.  DO NOT: Re-ask fields already captured. Push on gender if declined. Make referral source feel mandatory.  NEXT: All fields filled -\> ONBOARD-02 (experience fork).

VOICE UX RULES (apply to all agent screens):
- Section 2.4: Mic stays listening once granted. No re-tapping per utterance.
- Section 2.5: Voice drives navigation. Agent calls navigate_next when step complete. No button taps in voice mode. Buttons are fallback for text mode.
- ai_output_mode: If 'voice' = AI speaks via TTS. If 'screen' = AI writes text. Both modes work.
- Coaching style: Warm & Thoughtful only for MVP.
ONBOARD-01.AI Voice (TTS copy): AGENT INTRODUCTION (real-time TTS, NOT pre-recorded): "OK - let me get to know you a little. What's your name, how old are you, how do you identify, and how did you hear about us? You can just say it all or fill it in on screen."
ONBOARD-01.Voice Instructions: \*\*\* REAL-TIME AGENT - NOT PRE-RECORDED MP3 \*\*\* \[This screen uses the Cartesia Line agent, not an MP3 file\] \[Agent speaks the introduction automatically on screen load\] \[Agent responds to user speech in real-time via: STT \> GPT \> TTS\] \[Agent says the user's name back through live TTS cloned voice\] \[Form fields auto-fill as the user's speech is transcribed\] \[If mic denied: introduction displays as text bubble, user fills form manually\]
ONBOARD-01.Expected User Response: FULL: "I'm Sarah, 32, female, I found you on Instagram."  PARTIAL: "Sarah, 32." / "My name's Mike." / "I'm 28."  VARIATIONS: "Call me Sam" = name: Sam "Twenty-five" / "25" = age: 25 "Guy" / "man" / "boy" = Male "Girl" / "woman" / "lady" = Female "Non-binary" = Other "TikTok" / "IG" = Social media "A friend" = Friend  OR Tap: Fill form manually
ONBOARD-01.AI Response: \*\*\* Agent responds via real-time TTS (cloned voice) \*\*\*  FULL (user gave everything): "Great to meet you, Sarah. Let's build something that actually works for you." The agent says 'Sarah' (or whatever name the user gave) through live TTS. This is the moment - the AI coach knows their name and speaks it back.  PARTIAL (name + age only): "Great to meet you, Sarah. And how do you identify? Also curious how you heard about us - you can just tap those on screen if you prefer."  NAME ONLY: "Hey \[Name\]. And how old are you?"  NO NAME YET: "What should I call you?"  ALL VIA TAP (no voice): Agent does not speak a response. Form submission proceeds silently to ONBOARD-02.
ONBOARD-01.System Action: 1\. Parse voice with NLP: extract {name, age, gender, referral\_source} 2\. Auto-fill form fields in real-time 3\. On 'Let's Begin': validate all fields filled 4\. Save to Supabase user\_profile 5\. Navigate to ONBOARD-02 6\. Log PostHog: complete\_profile {input\_method, fields\_via\_voice}
ONBOARD-01.Edge Cases: PARTIAL: AI asks for remaining fields only. Does NOT re-ask what it got. AMBIGUOUS AGE: "Did you say 30 or 13? Just want to make sure." UNCLEAR GENDER: "I didn't catch how you identify. Male, female, or other?" NO REFERRAL: Leave as 'Other' - not critical. NAME UNCLEAR: "Could you say your name one more time?"
ONBOARD-01.Notes: FIRST REAL-TIME AGENT SCREEN. Voice auto-fills form in real-time - magic moment \#1. Agent says the user's name back via live TTS - magic moment \#2. This is the key test for the real-time pipeline: User speaks \> STT transcribes \> GPT generates response with name \> TTS speaks it in cloned voice.  NAME PRONUNCIATION: Common names (Sarah, Mike, Tim) will pronounce correctly. Uncommon names may be mispronounced by Cartesia TTS. Known limitation for MVP. Cartesia supports custom pronunciation via IPA notation and pronunciation dictionaries - can be addressed in Phase 2.

ECHECK-02.Notes: NLP must handle synonyms: gym=workout=exercise, reading=read=book, etc.
ECHECK-02.How Audio Works: Agent reads habits one by one. Real-time per habit.

ECHECK-03.AI Context: SCREEN: Evening AI-Read (coach reads habit list) STATE: User chose AI-read method.  BEHAVIOR: Go through habits one by one. Vary phrasing: 'Did you do it?' / 'How about \[habit\]?' / 'Last one - \[habit\]?' Responses per habit: Completed = 'Nice.' / Skipped = 'OK.' / Sort of = 'I'll mark that done. Every bit counts.' If user gives all at once ('did 1, 2, 4, skipped 3'), accept it - don't force one-by-one.  DO NOT: Be robotic. Guilt on skips. Extend responses beyond one word per habit.
ECHECK-03.AI Voice (TTS copy): "OK. Let's go through them.  \[Habit 1 name\] - did you do it?" \[After response, Habit 2:\] "How about \[habit name\]?" \[After response, Habit 3:\] "And \[habit name\]?" \[Last habit:\] "Last one - \[habit name\]?"  \[After all:\] "That's all of them. \[Summary\]."
ECHECK-03.Voice Instructions: \[Dynamic - iterates through user's active habits for today\] \[Vary phrasing per habit to avoid robotic repetition\] \[LLM-generated at runtime, not pre-cached TTS\]
ECHECK-03.Expected User Response: PER HABIT: "Yes" / "Yeah" / "Did it" / "Checked" "No" / "Skipped it" / "Didn't get to it" "Sort of" / "Half" / "Started but didn't finish"
ECHECK-03.AI Response: COMPLETED: "Nice." or "Got it." \[move to next\]  SKIPPED: "OK." \[move to next - no guilt\]  SORT OF: "I'll mark that as done. Every bit counts."  \[After last habit:\] "That's all of them. \[Summary\]."  \[Beat/pause before goal check or reflection:\] If goal exists: "One more thing..." then transition to ECHECK-04 If no goal but reflection configured: "Now let's do your reflection." -\> ECHECK-05 If neither: go straight to ECHECK-06 wrap-up
ECHECK-03.System Action: 1\. Iterate through today's habits one by one 2\. Wait for response after each 3\. Save status per response 4\. After all: summarize and transition to ECHECK-04/05
ECHECK-03.Edge Cases: User interrupts: "Actually I did \[habit 3\] too" mid-list AI: "Got it - marking that done. OK, next - \[continue list\]"  User wants to speed up: "I did 1, 2, and 4, skipped 3" AI: "Got it all. \[Summary\]." Don't force one-by-one if they gave everything.
ECHECK-03.Notes: Pace should be conversational, not robotic.

ECHECK-04.Screen / State: Goal check (only if morning goal set)
ECHECK-04.AI Context: SCREEN: Goal Check (evening, if morning goal exists) STATE: User set a morning goal. Now checking in on it.  BEHAVIOR: 'You said this morning you wanted to \[goal\]. How'd that go?' Achieved: attribute to user. Missed: 'No stress. Tomorrow's a fresh one.' Partial: 'Progress counts.'  DO NOT: Guilt. Validate excuses. Push back. Extended commentary.
ECHECK-04.AI Voice (TTS copy): "You said this morning you wanted to \[goal\_text\]. How'd that go?"
ECHECK-04.Expected User Response: ACHIEVED: "I did it" / "Yeah\!" / "Nailed it" / "Got it done"  MISSED: "No" / "Didn't happen" / "Not today"  PARTIAL: "Sort of" / "I started it" / "Got halfway"
ECHECK-04.AI Response: ACHIEVED: "You said you wanted to \[goal\] and you did. That's you following through."  MISSED: "No stress. Tomorrow's a fresh one."  PARTIAL: "Progress counts. You moved it forward."
ECHECK-04.System Action: 1\. Update daily\_goals.status = achieved/missed/partial 2\. Transition to ECHECK-05 if reflection configured 3\. Otherwise: ECHECK-06 (wrap up)
ECHECK-04.Edge Cases: Emotional response ("I'm so proud of myself"): AI: "You should be. That was all you."  Excuse-heavy response: AI does NOT validate excuses or push back. Just acknowledges and moves on.
ECHECK-04.Notes: Evening goal reference = the payoff of the morning goal question.

ECHECK-05.Screen Text (Figma): \[Journal prompts displayed one at a time\]
ECHECK-05.AI Context: SCREEN: Daily Reflection (mandatory) STATE: Habits reviewed. Now the reflection portion of evening check-in.  BEHAVIOR: Check user\_profile.reflection\_style: \- GUIDED: Three fixed prompts one at a time (proud, forgive, grateful). Brief 1-sentence reflection after each. \- CUSTOM: User's custom\_prompts\[\] one at a time. Same brief reflection pattern. \- FREEFORM: Open prompt ('How was your day?'). One reflection. Close.  After final answer (all styles): 'Thank you for sharing that.' Then transition silently to ECHECK-06 for the evening wrap-up.  IF 'Nothing' / 'I don't know': 'That's OK. We can move to the next one.' Do NOT push. IF emotional: Brief acknowledgment only. Do NOT therapize. IF user wants to skip tonight: 'No problem. We'll do it next time.' -\> wrap up.  DO NOT: Give long reflections. Ask follow-up questions. Steal the moment.
ECHECK-05.AI Voice (TTS copy): IF GUIDED: "Now let's do your reflection. What are you proud of today?" \[After answer:\] "\[Brief reflection\]. Next - what do you forgive yourself for today?" \[After answer:\] "\[Brief reflection\]. Last one - what are you grateful for?" \[After answer:\] "Thank you for sharing that."  IF CUSTOM: "Time for your reflection. \[User's first custom prompt\]?" \[After answer:\] "\[Brief reflection\]. \[User's second custom prompt\]?" \[Continue for all custom prompts\] \[After final:\] "Thank you for sharing that."  IF FREEFORM: "Time for your reflection. How was your day? Just talk about whatever comes to mind." \[After answer:\] "\[Brief reflection\]. Thank you for sharing that."
ECHECK-05.Voice Instructions: \[Dynamic - LLM generates based on reflection\_style\] \[Guided: 3 fixed prompts. Custom: user's prompts from custom\_prompts\[\]. Freeform: open-ended.\] \[Brief reflections between prompts are LLM-generated, not pre-cached\]
ECHECK-05.Expected User Response: Answers to each prompt - open-ended, emotional, personal.  Some people will give short answers: "Nothing" / "I don't know"  Some will give long answers.
ECHECK-05.AI Response: \[After each answer - brief, meaningful\]  Prompt 1 answer: "\[Brief reflection\]. Next - what do you forgive yourself for today?"  Prompt 2 answer: "\[Brief reflection\]. Last one - what are you grateful for?"  Prompt 3 answer: "Thank you for sharing that. See you tomorrow morning, \[Name\]."
ECHECK-05.System Action: 1\. Save each response to Supabase journal\_entries {user\_id, date, prompt, response, input\_method} 2\. After all prompts: transition to ECHECK-06 3\. Log PostHog: journal\_entry {prompt\_count, total\_length, input\_method}
ECHECK-05.Edge Cases: "Nothing" / "I don't know": AI: "That's OK. Sometimes nothing comes to mind. We can move to the next one." Do NOT push.  User gets emotional: AI: Brief acknowledgment. "That sounds like it matters a lot." Do NOT therapize.  User wants to skip reflection tonight: "No problem. We'll do it next time." -\> skip to wrap up
ECHECK-05.Notes: AI responses to reflections should be brief - 1 sentence. Don't steal the moment.
ECHECK-05.How Audio Works: Agent reads prompts, captures voice responses.

ECHECK-06.AI Context: SCREEN: Evening Wrap-up STATE: Habits reviewed, goal checked, reflection done. Last voice before bed.  BEHAVIOR: Summarize: '\[X\] out of \[Y\] habits today. \[Brief comment\]. See you tomorrow morning, \[Name\].' All done = 'Full day. Rest well.' Most = 'Solid day.' Few = 'Some days are like that. Tomorrow's fresh.' None = 'That's OK. Tomorrow we start again. Sleep well.'  DO NOT: Pile on if bad day + bad mood. Be preachy. End on anything but warmth.
ECHECK-06.AI Voice (TTS copy): Example: "Three out of four habits today. Solid day. Rest well, \[Name\]."  ALL COMPLETE: "All habits done today. That's a full day. Rest well." MOST: "Three out of four - solid day. Rest well, \[Name\]." FEW: "One out of four today. Some days are like that. Tomorrow's fresh." NONE: "Sometimes the day doesn't go as planned. That's OK. Tomorrow we start again. Sleep well, \[Name\]."
ECHECK-06.Voice Instructions: \[Dynamic - LLM generates based on habit completion count\] \[Template: '\[completed\] out of \[total\] habits today. \[Comment\]. See you tomorrow morning, \[Name\].'\] \[Not pre-cached TTS - varies daily\]
ECHECK-06.AI Response: ALL COMPLETE: "All habits done today. That's a full day. Rest well."   MOST DONE: "Three out of four - solid day. Rest well, \[Name\]."  FEW DONE: "One out of four today. Some days are like that. Tomorrow's fresh."  NONE DONE: "Sometimes the day doesn't go as planned. That's OK. Tomorrow we start again. Sleep well, \[Name\]."
ECHECK-06.System Action: 1\. Calculate daily completion rate 2\. Update streak data 3\. Return to Home 4\. Log PostHog: evening\_complete {habits\_completed, habits\_total, had\_goal, goal\_status, reflection\_done}
ECHECK-06.Edge Cases: NONE done + bad mood from morning: AI does NOT pile on. Keep it gentle. "Rest well tonight. Tomorrow's a new start."  Perfect day + good mood: "Clean sweep today. You earned a good night's rest."
ECHECK-06.Notes: Last voice they hear before bed. Warm, brief, forward-looking.

VOICE-01.Screen / State: Voice conversation Mic tap from any screen
VOICE-01.AI Context: SCREEN: Voice Conversation (anytime, from any screen) STATE: User tapped mic from anywhere. Open-ended conversation.  BEHAVIOR: 'What's on your mind?' Determine intent: \- Habit creation: 'What habit would you like to add?' -\> collect name, time, freq, reminder. \- Habit edit: 'Done - \[change made\].' \- Coaching/stress: Acknowledge, don't fix. 'What's the main thing weighing on you?' \- Venting: 'I hear you.' Brief acknowledgment. Do NOT try to fix. \- Question about data: Answer from their data. \- Check-in request: Route to MCHECK-01 or ECHECK-01.  CONTEXT-AWARE: If from habit detail -\> 'Want to talk about \[habit\]?' If from focus session -\> 'Taking a break?' If from insights -\> 'Questions about your data?'  MENTAL HEALTH BOUNDARY: If user expresses self-harm, crisis, or suicidal thoughts: Stop coaching. Express care. Provide 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline. Do NOT continue normal conversation.  MULTI-TURN: Voice conversations can be back-and-forth within a session. Maintain context - if user asks a follow-up, reference what was just discussed. Session ends when user stops talking, taps away, or navigates to another screen.  CAP: 5 open-ended conversations/day. Check-ins don't count. Silent cap.  DO NOT: Give speeches (4 sentences max). Assume intent. Fix venting.
VOICE-01.Expected User Response: HABIT CREATION: "I want to start meditating" / "Add a new habit" / "I want to add another habit" / "Another habit" / "One more habit" / "Can I add a habit?"  HABIT EDIT: "Change my gym time to mornings"  COACHING: "I've been stressed" / "I'm not motivated"  QUESTION: "How many habits have I completed this week?"  VENTING: "Today was awful" / long emotional share  CHECK-IN: "Let me do my check-in" -\> route to check-in  GENERAL: "What can you do?" / "Help"
VOICE-01.AI Response: \[AI generates response based on intent + coaching style\]  HABIT (with name): "\[habit\] - great idea. Let me set that up. What time, how often, and want a reminder?"  HABIT (no name, e.g. 'add another habit' / 'another habit' / 'one more'): "Sure - what habit would you like to add?" Then once they give the name: "Got it - \[habit\]. What time, how often, and want a reminder?"  EDIT: "Done - \[change made\]."  STRESS: "That sounds heavy. What's the main thing weighing on you?"  VENTING: "I hear you. \[Brief acknowledgment - do NOT try to fix\]."  QUESTION: "\[Answer from data\]."  HELP / WHAT CAN YOU DO: "I can help you add or change habits, talk through how your day's going, answer questions about your progress, or just listen. What do you need?"
VOICE-01.System Action: 1\. Capture voice via STT 2\. Send to LLM with context (habits, recent check-ins, goals, coaching style) 3\. LLM determines intent 4\. Route by intent: habit CRUD, coaching, data query, general 5\. Increment daily\_voice\_count 6\. If count \>= 5: see VOICE-CAP 7\. Log PostHog: voice\_conversation {intent, duration, actions\_taken}
VOICE-01.Edge Cases: CHECK-INS DO NOT COUNT toward voice conversation cap.  CONTEXT-AWARE: If mic tapped from habit detail screen, AI knows which habit: "Want to talk about \[habit name\]?"  From focus session: "Taking a break from your focus session?"  From insights: "Questions about your data?"  MENTAL HEALTH BOUNDARY: If user says something concerning (self-harm, crisis): AI: "I hear you, and I want you to know that what you're feeling matters. I'm an AI coach and I'm not equipped to help with this the way you deserve. Please reach out to \[crisis resource\] or talk to someone you trust." Do NOT continue coaching. Do NOT minimize.
VOICE-01.Notes: Context changes based on which screen mic was tapped from.  Cap is silent - no counter shown.  CRITICAL: Mental health boundary response. If user expresses self-harm, suicidal thoughts, or crisis: stop coaching, express care, provide 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline (call/text 988). Do NOT continue normal conversation.
VOICE-01.How Audio Works: Free-form conversation. Agent determines intent. Real-time.

VOICE-CAP.AI Context: SCREEN: Voice Cap Reached STATE: User has used 5 voice conversations today.  BEHAVIOR: 'You've used your voice sessions for today. I'll be here tomorrow. You can still do your check-ins and log habits on screen.'  WHAT COUNTS: Only open-ended conversations. Check-ins, focus, habit edits = free.  DO NOT: Show a counter. Make it feel punitive. Block check-ins.
VOICE-CAP.AI Voice (TTS copy): "You've used your voice sessions for today. I'll be here tomorrow. In the meantime, you can still do your check-ins and log habits on screen."
VOICE-CAP.Expected User Response: User might try again: same message. User might ask why: same message.
VOICE-CAP.System Action: 1\. Check daily\_voice\_count \>= 5 (excluding check-ins) 2\. Show gentle message 3\. Disable mic for conversations (keep for check-ins) 4\. Reset at midnight user's timezone
VOICE-CAP.Edge Cases: Check-ins NEVER count. Focus start/end don't count. Habit edit via voice doesn't count. Only open-ended conversations count.
VOICE-CAP.How Audio Works: voice_cap.mp3. "You've used your voice sessions for today."

"Want to talk about \[habit name\]?"
